name: Smith Machine Romanian Deadlift
resistance_profile: The fixed bar path loads the hamstrings hardest in the stretched bottom position while removing balance demands, letting you safely push the loaded stretch.
cues: Hips back, bar glued to the thighs
Flat back — chase the stretch, not the floor
Stand tall and squeeze, don't hyperextend
execution_steps: Set the Smith bar to about mid-thigh height, load it, then grip it shoulder-width and unrack, standing so the bar rests against your thighs.
Soften the knees and push your hips straight back, letting the bar slide down the fixed path close to your legs.
Lower until you feel a strong hamstring stretch, keeping a flat back and the bar tracking the legs.
Drive your hips forward to stand tall, squeeze the glutes at the top, and re-rack after the set.
good_when: You are new to hinging and want the stability of a fixed bar path
You want to overload the stretched position without balance limiting you
You train alone and want a safe way to push heavier RDLs
bad_when: You want to train balance and stabilizers (use a free barbell)
The fixed vertical path fights your natural hinge groove
You already have the skill for free-weight RDLs and want maximal transfer
common_errors: Bending the knees into a squat
Rounding the lower back to go deeper
Standing too far from the bar so it drifts off the legs
progressions: Add load, then progress to a free-barbell Romanian deadlift
